tool.name: bruteforce_directories
tool.description:
Perform async directory and file brute-forcing.
Enumerates directories and files on a web server using wordlists, with smart detection of custom 404 pages and interesting responses.
This is ACTIVE reconnaissance - it makes HTTP requests to discover hidden content, admin panels, backup files, and sensitive paths.
Features: - Async concurrent requests for speed - Custom 404 detection (handles soft 404s) - Stealth mode for careful enumeration - Extension fuzzing (.php, .bak, .old, etc.) - Categorizes findings by type
Common Findings: - Admin panels (/admin, /dashboard, /cpanel) - Backup files (.sql, .zip, .bak) - Config files (.env, config.php) - API endpoints (/api, /graphql) - Version control (.git, .svn) - Debug endpoints (/debug, /phpinfo.php)

parameters.parameter: Path to custom wordlist file (one path per line)
Maximum concurrent requests (reduced in stealth mode)
Custom list of paths to test
Status codes to exclude from results (e.g., [404, 500])

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - success: Whether scan completed - url: Target URL - found: List of discovered paths with status codes and metadata - found_count: Number of paths found - statistics: Scan statistics (time, rate, etc.) - by_status: Breakdown by HTTP status code - by_category: Breakdown by finding category

tool.name: smart_path_gen
tool.description:
Generate intelligent path suggestions based on context.
Analyzes the target and generates likely paths based on: - Detected technologies (PHP, Java, Python, etc.) - CMS platform (WordPress, Drupal, Joomla) - Domain name patterns - Existing findings for pattern learning
Use this before brute-forcing to create a targeted wordlist.
Path Categories: - high_priority: Always-check paths (.env, .git, robots.txt) - tech_specific: Based on tech stack - cms_specific: Based on CMS platform - pattern_based: Learned from existing findings - sensitive: Domain-specific backup/config files

parameters.parameter: Already discovered paths for pattern learning

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - suggestions: Paths organized by category - all_paths: Combined list of all suggestions - total_count: Total paths generated - by_category: Count per category

tool.name: recursive_dir_scan
tool.description:
Recursively scan discovered directories.
After initial enumeration finds directories, this tool recursively explores them to find deeper content.
Use this when: - Initial scan found interesting directories - You need to map the full directory structure - Looking for files nested deep in the tree
Warning: Can generate many requests. Use stealth mode and low max_depth for sensitive targets.

parameters.parameter: Target directory URL to scan recursively

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - found: All discovered paths - found_count: Total paths found - directories_scanned: Number of directories explored - max_depth_reached: Depth limit used

tool.name: comprehensive_dir_enum
tool.description:
Perform comprehensive directory enumeration.
This is the RECOMMENDED entry point for directory discovery. It orchestrates the full pipeline:
1. Smart path generation based on tech stack and CMS 2. Initial directory brute-forcing with combined wordlist 3. Optional recursive scanning of discovered directories 4. Categorization and prioritization of findings
Use This When: - Starting reconnaissance on a web application - You want intelligent path suggestions - You need categorized, prioritized results - You want recursive directory exploration
After This Tool: - Add discovered endpoints to coverage tracker - Create hypotheses for admin panels, API endpoints - Test sensitive files for information disclosure - Fuzz discovered forms and parameters

parameters.parameter: Detected technologies for smart path generation

tool.returns: Comprehensive results including: - findings: All discovered paths - priority_findings: High-priority findings (admin, config, etc.) - by_category: Findings organized by type - categories: Detailed category breakdown - statistics: Scan statistics
